text: We think that if we just meditated enough or jogged enough or ate perfect food, everything would be perfect. But from the point of view of someone who is awake, that’s death. Seeking security or perfection, rejoicing in feeling confirmed and whole, self contained and comfortable, is some kind of death. It doesn’t have any fresh air. There’s no room for something to come in and interrupt all that. We are killing the moment by controlling our experience.
author: Pema Chödrön
book_title: When Things Fall Apart: Heart Advice for Difficult Times

text: it is impossible to build one's own happiness on the unhappiness of others. This perspective is at the heart of Buddhist teachings.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: Man suffers only because he takes seriously what the gods made for fun.
author: Alan Wilson Watts

text: When the mind is exhausted of images, it invents its own.
author: Gary Snyder
book_title: Earth House Hold: Technical Notes & Queries to Fellow Dharma Revolutionaries

text: Even if things don't unfold the way you expected, don't be disheartened or give up. One who continues to advance will win in the end.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: Life is painful. It has thorns, like the stem of a rose. Culture and art are the roses that bloom on the stem. The flower is yourself, your humanity. Art is the liberation of the humanity inside yourself.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: The Way is not in the sky; the Way is in the heart.
author: Gautama Buddha

text: Many people think excitement is happiness.... But when you are excited you are not peaceful. True happiness is based on peace.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text:
Too lazy to be ambitious,
I let the world take care of itself.
Ten days' worth of rice in my bag;
a bundle of twigs by the fireplace.
Why chatter about delusion and enlightenment?
Listening to the night rain on my roof,
I sit comfortably, with both legs stretched out.
author: Ryōkan

text: Usually when we hear or read something new, we just compare it to our own ideas. If it is the same, we accept it and say that it is correct. If it is not, we say it is incorrect. In either case, we learn nothing.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
book_title: The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching: Transforming Suffering into Peace, Joy, and Liberation

text: It is my conviction that there is no way to peace - peace is the way.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Not thinking about anything is Zen. Once you know this, walking, sitting, or lying down, everything you do is Zen.
author: Bodhidharma

text: To dwell in the here and now does not mean you never think about the past or responsibly plan for the future. The idea is simply not to allow yourself to get lost in regrets about the past or worries about the future. If you are firmly grounded in the present moment, the past can be an object of inquiry, the object of your mindfulness and concentration. You can attain many insights by looking into the past. But you are still grounded in the present moment.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text:
Where there are humans,
You'll find flies,
And Buddhas.
author: Kobayashi Issa

text: The institutions of human society treat us as parts of a machine. They assign us ranks and place considerable pressure upon us to fulfill defined roles. We need something to help us restore our lost and distorted humanity. Each of us has feelings that have been suppressed and have built up inside. There is a voiceless cry resting in the depths of our souls, waiting for expression. Art gives the soul's feelings voice and form.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text:
A student, filled with emotion and crying, implored, "Why is there so much suffering?"
Suzuki Roshi replied, "No reason.
author: Shunryu Suzuki
book_title: Zen Is Right Here: Teaching Stories and Anecdotes of Shunryu Suzuki, Author of "ZEN Mind, Beginner's Mind"

text: Walk as if you are kissing the Earth with your feet.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
book_title: Peace Is Every Step: The Path of Mindfulness in Everyday Life

text: Letting go gives us freedom, and freedom is the only condition for happiness. If, in our heart, we still cling to anything - anger, anxiety, or possessions - we cannot be free.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
book_title: The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching: Transforming Suffering into Peace, Joy, and Liberation

text: Maybe love at first sight isn’t what we think it is. Maybe it’s recognising a soul we loved in a past life and falling in love with them again.
author: Kamand Kojouri

text: Learning to let go should be learned before learning to get. Life should be touched, not strangled. You’ve got to relax, let it happen at times, and at others move forward with it.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: A fool sees himself as another, but a wise man sees others as himself.
author: Dōgen
book_title: How to Cook Your Life: From the Zen Kitchen to Enlightenment

text: A great human revolution in just a single individual will help achieve a change in the destiny of a nation and, further, can even enable a change in the destiny of all humankind.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: The secret of Buddhism is to remove all ideas, all concepts, in order for the truth to have a chance to penetrate, to reveal itself.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
book_title: Buddha Mind, Buddha Body: Walking Toward Enlightenment

text: Being vegetarian here also means that we do not consume dairy and egg products, because they are products of the meat industry. If we stop consuming, they will stop producing. Only collective awakening can create enough determination for action.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
book_title: The Fruitful Darkness: A Journey Through Buddhist Practice and Tribal Wisdom

text: When you catch yourself slipping into a pool of negativity, notice how it derives from nothing other than resistance to the current situation.
author: Donna Quesada
book_title: Buddha in the Classroom: Zen Wisdom to Inspire Teachers
